claims.text: For elite teams, the lead time for changes is less than one hour.

evidence_spans.text: For elite teams the lead time for changes is less than one hour.

claims.text: The mainline should always be kept in a deployable state.

evidence_spans.text: A core principle of continuous delivery is to keep the mainline always in a deployable state.

claims.text: Short-lived feature branches should live for no more than a couple of days before being merged.

evidence_spans.text: Short-lived feature branches are permitted but should live for no more than a couple of days before being merged.

claims.text: A blameless postmortem focuses on the systemic causes of failure rather than blaming individuals, so that people share information openly.

evidence_spans.text: A blameless postmortem focuses on the systemic causes of failure rather than blaming individuals, so that people share information openly.

claims.text: The single-team method is designed for one team of collaborating developers.

evidence_spans.text: The single-team method is designed for one team of collaborating developers.

claims.text: A feature flag is a configuration switch that lets a team turn a feature on or off at runtime without deploying new code, decoupling deployment of code from release of functionality.

evidence_spans.text: A feature flag is a configuration switch that lets a team turn a feature on or off at runtime without deploying new code. Feature flags decouple deployment of code from release of functionality.

claims.text: The zero technical debt policy forbids commented-out code, placeholder TODO markers, and unjustified suppression of warnings.

evidence_spans.text: There must be no commented-out code, no placeholder TODO markers, and no unjustified suppression of warnings.

claims.text: Continuous delivery treats every change that passes the automated tests as a release candidate.

evidence_spans.text: Every change that passes the automated tests is a release candidate.

claims.text: The solo SDLC method is described in sdlc-solo.md in the eval-suite library.

evidence_spans.text: The solo method is the lightest of the four SDLC methods and is designed for a single developer working alone.

claims.text: Phase gates for multiple teams are described in sdlc-programme.md; the programme method introduces formal waterfall phase gates for requirements, design, test, and code, with four phase-gate validators that enforce the gates automatically.

evidence_spans.text: It introduces formal waterfall phase gates for requirements, design, test, and code.
Four phase-gate validators enforce the gates automatically.
